# PolyDot — Complete Build Specification
# AI Instruction Document: Logic, Architecture, UX, Testing
# NO CODE IN THIS FILE — pure logic, decisions, and verified references

---

## MANDATORY: READ ALL REFERENCED DOCS BEFORE WRITING ANY CODE

Every technical claim is sourced from official Polkadot docs.
Before writing a single line of code, fetch and read every page below:

1. https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/get-started/
2. https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/for-eth-devs/evm-vs-pvm/
3. https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/connect/
4. https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/precompiles/
5. https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/precompiles/system/
6. https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/precompiles/erc20/
7. https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/dev-environments/hardhat/
8. https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/libraries/wagmi/
9. https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/integrations/wallets/
10. https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/explorers/

---

## SECTION 1 — WHAT WE ARE BUILDING AND WHY

### Project Name
PolyDot — Prediction Market Protocol on Polkadot Hub

### One-Line Description
A decentralized YES/NO prediction market where anyone creates questions,
anyone bets native PAS on outcomes, and resolution happens through an
optimistic dispute system anchored to Polkadot's on-chain identity layer.

### Hackathon Track
Track 2 — PVM Smart Contract
Categories covered:
  A) Applications using Polkadot native Assets
  B) Accessing Polkadot native functionality via precompiles

### Why This Is Genuinely Track 2 and Not Track 1

Track 1 = Solidity compiled normally, standard EVM execution, standard tokens.
Track 2 = Solidity compiled via resolc to RISC-V, runs on PolkaVM,
          uses Polkadot-runtime-exclusive capabilities.

PolyDot uses two things impossible on any standard EVM chain:

CAPABILITY 1 — Native PAS bets:
Bets are placed using msg.value in PAS, Polkadot Hub's runtime-level native
asset. Not wrapped, not bridged, not a deployed ERC-20. It is the actual
asset managed by Polkadot's own runtime. Paying out also uses direct PAS
transfers — no token contract intermediary at any point.

CAPABILITY 2 — System Precompile (the real PVM differentiator):
Address: 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000900
This is part of Polkadot's runtime exposed to Solidity. Does not exist on
Ethereum, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, or any other EVM chain.

The specific function used: toAccountId(address) → bytes32
Converts an H160 EVM address into the 32-byte Polkadot Substrate AccountId.
This is the native identity bridge between the EVM world and Polkadot world.
Source: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/precompiles/system/

In PolyDot, every time someone proposes a market outcome, the contract calls
toAccountId on the proposer's EVM address and permanently logs their Polkadot
Substrate identity on-chain. Accountability is tied to a verifiable Polkadot
identity — not just an anonymous hex address. This is architecturally unique
to PolkaVM and cannot be replicated on any standard EVM chain.

### What Makes PolyDot Different From Every Current Submission

After analysing all 37 current hackathon submissions:
- Zero prediction markets exist in either track — zero competition
- No submission uses the System Precompile for identity verification as a product feature
- No submission creates something judges can actually participate in during the demo
- The optimistic resolution mechanism is novel for this ecosystem

---

## SECTION 2 — VERIFIED NETWORK DETAILS

Source: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/connect/

Do not assume or change these values. All verified from official docs.

Network name:   Polkadot Hub TestNet (Paseo Asset Hub)
RPC URL:        https://testnet-passet-hub-eth-rpc.polkadot.io
Chain ID:       420420417
Currency:       PAS
Decimals:       18
Explorer:       https://blockscout-testnet.polkadot.io
Faucet:         https://faucet.polkadot.io

Wallet support: MetaMask works via manual network addition.
Source: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/integrations/wallets/

---

## SECTION 3 — POLKAVM COMPILATION CONSTRAINTS

Source: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/for-eth-devs/evm-vs-pvm/
Read this page fully before writing any Solidity.

FORBIDDEN — do not use any of these:
  SELFDESTRUCT — not supported on PolkaVM
  EXTCODECOPY — not supported
  CODECOPY — not supported
  CALLCODE — not supported

SOLIDITY VERSION: Use 0.8.28

COMPILER: resolc (not standard solc). Hardhat plugin handles this automatically.
Source: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/dev-environments/hardhat/

OPENZEPPELIN: Use v4.x only if needed. v5.x is incompatible with resolc.
Recommendation: keep PolyDot.sol self-contained with no OZ inheritance to
avoid bytecode size limits and resolc compatibility issues.

TRANSFERS: Never use .transfer() or .send() for PAS.
They carry a 2300 gas stipend that fails on PolkaVM.
Always use low-level call pattern: (bool ok,) = addr.call{value: amount}("")
Always check: require(ok, "reason")

MEMORY: PolkaVM has tighter memory limits. No unbounded loops in state-changing
functions. getAllMarkets() view function is fine for testnet scale.

ASSEMBLY: Avoid unless necessary.

---

## SECTION 4 — SYSTEM PRECOMPILE DETAILS

Source: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/precompiles/system/
Read this full page before implementing anything that touches 0x900.

Address: 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000900

This precompile is part of pallet-revive, Polkadot Hub's runtime layer.
It exposes Polkadot-native runtime functions to Solidity.

FUNCTIONS USED IN POLYDOT:

toAccountId(address addr) → bytes32
  Converts H160 EVM address to 32-byte Substrate AccountId.
  Returns the SS58 pubkey bytes of the corresponding Polkadot account.
  Called in proposeOutcome() and disputeProposal() to record identity.

callerIsOrigin() → bool
  Returns true if msg.sender is the original transaction EOA, not a contract.
  Optional extra guard on propose/dispute to block contract-driven spam.

minimumBalance() → uint256
  Returns the existential deposit for an account on this chain.
  Used defensively to verify PROPOSAL_BOND is meaningfully above dust threshold.

LOCAL TESTING MOCK:
System Precompile is only available on the Polkadot Hub testnet.
For local Hardhat tests, deploy a MockSystem contract that implements the same
three functions with simple deterministic logic (e.g., toAccountId returns
bytes32 derived from the address, callerIsOrigin always returns true).
The mock is NEVER deployed to testnet.
The PolyDot contract accepts the precompile address as a constructor argument
so tests inject the mock and testnet always uses 0x900.

---

## SECTION 5 — COMPLETE CONTRACT LOGIC

### 5.1 Data Structures

ENUMS:

Outcome: NONE=0, YES=1, NO=2
  Bettor's chosen side, proposer's suggested outcome, market's final result.

MarketStatus: OPEN=0, CLOSED=1, PROPOSED=2, DISPUTED=3, RESOLVED=4, CANCELLED=5
  Every market moves strictly forward through these states. Never backward.
  All functions enforce the exact required status or revert.

STRUCTS:

Market:
  question (string) — the YES/NO question, max 280 chars
  creator (address) — market creator, acts as tiebreaker on disputes
  deadline (uint256) — unix timestamp when betting closes
  yesPool (uint256) — total PAS in wei bet on YES
  noPool (uint256) — total PAS in wei bet on NO
  createdAt (uint256) — block.timestamp at creation
  status (MarketStatus enum)
  result (Outcome enum) — only meaningful when RESOLVED

Proposal:
  proposer (address)
  proposedOutcome (Outcome)
  proposedAt (uint256) — block.timestamp when proposal was made
  disputed (bool) — true if someone challenged
  disputer (address) — zero if undisputed
  proposerSubstrateId (bytes32) — from System Precompile toAccountId(proposer)
  disputerSubstrateId (bytes32) — from System Precompile toAccountId(disputer)
  NOTE: These substrate IDs are the core PVM feature. Permanently on-chain.

Position:
  side (Outcome) — YES or NO
  amount (uint256) — PAS bet in wei
  claimed (bool) — prevents double-claim

MAPPINGS:
  markets: uint256 → Market
  proposals: uint256 → Proposal
  positions: uint256 marketId → address bettor → Position

STATE VARIABLES:
  marketCount: uint256, starts at 0, increments per createMarket call
  feeRecipient: address, receives 1% platform fee on claim payouts
  systemPrecompile: address, injected via constructor

### 5.2 Constants

PROPOSAL_BOND = 0.05 ether
  PAS required to propose or dispute. Returned if correct. Slashed if wrong.

CHALLENGE_WINDOW = 2 hours (7200 seconds)
  After a proposal, this is how long the community has to dispute it.

CREATOR_WINDOW = 24 hours (86400 seconds)
  After a dispute, this is how long the creator has to give the final answer.

PLATFORM_FEE_BPS = 100 (= 1%)
  Basis points taken from winner payouts on claim. Sent to feeRecipient.

MIN_BET = 0.001 ether
  Minimum bet. Prevents dust positions that cost more gas to claim than they're worth.

MAX_QUESTION_LENGTH = 280 chars

### 5.3 Events

Every state change must emit an event. The frontend reads all of these.

MarketCreated(uint256 indexed id, address indexed creator, string question, uint256 deadline)
BetPlaced(uint256 indexed marketId, address indexed bettor, uint8 side, uint256 amount)
MarketClosed(uint256 indexed marketId)
OutcomeProposed(uint256 indexed marketId, address indexed proposer, uint8 outcome, bytes32 proposerSubstrateId)
OutcomeDisputed(uint256 indexed marketId, address indexed disputer, bytes32 disputerSubstrateId)
MarketResolved(uint256 indexed marketId, uint8 result, address resolvedBy)
MarketCancelled(uint256 indexed marketId, string reason)
Claimed(uint256 indexed marketId, address indexed user, uint256 amount)
BondReturned(uint256 indexed marketId, address indexed to, uint256 amount)

### 5.4 Function Logic — Every Function

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
CONSTRUCTOR(feeRecipient, systemPrecompile)
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Stores both addresses. Reverts if either is zero address. Nothing else.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
createMarket(question, deadline) → marketId
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Validations:
  question must not be empty string
  question.length must be ≤ 280
  deadline must be > block.timestamp + 1 hour
  deadline must be < block.timestamp + 365 days

Logic:
  marketId = marketCount, then marketCount++
  Create Market with status=OPEN, result=NONE, both pools at 0
  Store in markets[marketId]
  Emit MarketCreated

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
bet(marketId, side) payable
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
side: uint8 where 1=YES, 2=NO

Validations:
  status must be OPEN
  block.timestamp must be < market.deadline
  side must be 1 or 2 (not 0)
  msg.value must be ≥ MIN_BET
  positions[marketId][msg.sender].amount must be 0 (no double-bet)

Logic:
  Add msg.value to yesPool or noPool accordingly
  Store Position{side, amount=msg.value, claimed=false}
  Emit BetPlaced

Money safety: PAS from msg.value stays in contract. No external transfer here.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
closeMarket(marketId)
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Anyone can call after deadline. No access control.

Validations:
  status must be OPEN
  block.timestamp must be ≥ market.deadline

Three branches:

BRANCH A — No bets on either side:
  status = CANCELLED
  Emit MarketCancelled("No bets placed")

BRANCH B — Only one side has bets:
  status = CANCELLED
  Emit MarketCancelled("Only one side has bets")
  Bettors can claim() for full refund

BRANCH C — Both sides have bets:
  status = CLOSED
  Emit MarketClosed

Rationale for B: A real market needs two sides. Returning money is correct.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
proposeOutcome(marketId, outcome) payable
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
THE CORE PVM FEATURE FUNCTION.
outcome: uint8 where 1=YES, 2=NO

Validations:
  status must be CLOSED
  outcome must be 1 or 2
  msg.value must be exactly PROPOSAL_BOND
  proposals[marketId].proposer must be address(0) — no existing proposal

PVM STEP (must happen before storing proposal):
  Call ISystem(systemPrecompile).toAccountId(msg.sender)
  Returns bytes32 — the proposer's Polkadot Substrate AccountId
  This call goes to precompile at 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000900
  On local tests it goes to MockSystem instead

Logic:
  Store substrateId returned from precompile
  Create Proposal struct with all fields including proposerSubstrateId
  Set market.status = PROPOSED
  Emit OutcomeProposed(marketId, msg.sender, outcome, substrateId)

The substrateId is permanently in the event log and in contract storage.
The proposer's Polkadot identity is now provably attached to this claim.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
disputeProposal(marketId) payable
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Validations:
  status must be PROPOSED
  proposal.disputed must be false
  msg.sender must NOT be proposal.proposer
  block.timestamp must be ≤ proposal.proposedAt + CHALLENGE_WINDOW
  msg.value must be exactly PROPOSAL_BOND

PVM STEP (same pattern as propose):
  Call ISystem(systemPrecompile).toAccountId(msg.sender)
  Store substrateId as disputerSubstrateId

Logic:
  Update proposal: disputed=true, disputer=msg.sender, disputerSubstrateId=substrateId
  Set market.status = DISPUTED
  Emit OutcomeDisputed(marketId, msg.sender, substrateId)

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
finalizeUndisputed(marketId)
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Anyone can call after challenge window closes if no dispute was raised.

Validations:
  status must be PROPOSED
  proposal.disputed must be false
  block.timestamp must be > proposal.proposedAt + CHALLENGE_WINDOW

Logic:
  Set market.result = proposal.proposedOutcome
  Set market.status = RESOLVED
  Return PROPOSAL_BOND to proposer via low-level call
  Emit BondReturned
  Emit MarketResolved(marketId, result, proposal.proposer)

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
creatorResolveDispute(marketId, finalOutcome)
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Validations:
  status must be DISPUTED
  msg.sender must be market.creator
  finalOutcome must be 1 or 2

Two branches:

If finalOutcome == proposal.proposedOutcome (proposer was right):
  Return PROPOSAL_BOND to proposer
  Send disputer's PROPOSAL_BOND to feeRecipient (disputer loses bond)

If finalOutcome != proposal.proposedOutcome (disputer was right):
  Return PROPOSAL_BOND to disputer
  Send proposer's PROPOSAL_BOND to feeRecipient (proposer loses bond)

Always:
  Set market.result = finalOutcome
  Set market.status = RESOLVED
  Emit MarketResolved

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
cancelExpiredDispute(marketId)
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
If creator ignores a dispute past CREATOR_WINDOW, anyone can protect bettors.

Validations:
  status must be DISPUTED
  block.timestamp must be > proposal.proposedAt + CHALLENGE_WINDOW + CREATOR_WINDOW

Logic:
  Set market.status = CANCELLED
  Send both bonds (2 × PROPOSAL_BOND) to feeRecipient
  Emit MarketCancelled("Creator did not resolve dispute")

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
claim(marketId)
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Validations (in order):
  positions[marketId][msg.sender].amount must be > 0
  positions[marketId][msg.sender].claimed must be false

Set claimed = true FIRST before any transfer (CEI — reentrancy protection)

BRANCH — CANCELLED market:
  Transfer exact position.amount back to msg.sender (full refund, no fee)
  Emit Claimed

BRANCH — RESOLVED market:
  If position.side != market.result: revert "You are on the losing side"
  Calculate payout:
    totalPool = yesPool + noPool
    winningPool = (result==YES) ? yesPool : noPool
    grossPayout = (position.amount × totalPool) / winningPool
    fee = (grossPayout × PLATFORM_FEE_BPS) / 10000
    netPayout = grossPayout - fee
  Transfer fee to feeRecipient
  Transfer netPayout to msg.sender
  Emit Claimed

BRANCH — Any other status: revert "Market not yet resolved"

Payout math example (for test verification):
  yesPool=3 PAS, noPool=1 PAS, user bet YES with 1 PAS
  grossPayout = (1 × 4) / 3 = 1.333 PAS (Solidity truncates to 1.333...)
  fee = 1% of 1.333 = 0.01333 PAS
  netPayout ≈ 1.32 PAS

Integer division truncation is expected and acceptable. The tiny remainder
stays in the contract. This must be documented in README.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
VIEW FUNCTIONS (no gas when called off-chain)
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
getMarket(marketId) → Market struct
getProposal(marketId) → Proposal struct
getPosition(marketId, address) → Position struct
getOdds(marketId) → (yesPct uint256, noPct uint256)
  Logic: if totalPool==0 return (50,50), else yesPct=(yesPool×100)/totalPool, noPct=100-yesPct
getAllMarkets() → Market[] array (index 0 to marketCount-1)
marketCount() → uint256 (auto-generated public getter)

---

## SECTION 6 — SECURITY PROPERTIES

CEI Pattern (Checks-Effects-Interactions):
  All checks (require) first → state updates → external transfers last.
  Applied consistently in every function that sends PAS.
  This prevents reentrancy without needing imported ReentrancyGuard.

No double bet: position.amount==0 check before writing new position.

No double claim: claimed=true written before any transfer in claim().

State machine: every function checks exact required status enum value.

Bond exactness: msg.value == PROPOSAL_BOND (not ≥, exactly ==).
Prevents accidental overpayment being locked in contract.

Transfer safety: all PAS sent via low-level call with require(ok) check.
Never .transfer(), never .send().

No tx.origin: never use tx.origin for authorization. If needed, use
ISystem.callerIsOrigin() from the System Precompile instead.

---

## SECTION 7 — TESTING REQUIREMENTS

Framework: Hardhat + @nomicfoundation/hardhat-toolbox + hardhat-network-helpers
Source: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/dev-environments/hardhat/

All tests run on local Hardhat network with MockSystem deployed.
MockSystem is injected as constructor arg — never hard-coded.

REQUIRED TEST GROUPS (45+ tests minimum):

GROUP 1 — Deployment (5 tests):
  Fee recipient stored correctly
  System precompile address stored correctly
  marketCount starts at 0
  Reverts if fee recipient is zero address
  Reverts if system precompile is zero address

GROUP 2 — createMarket (7 tests):
  Stores all fields correctly
  Increments marketCount
  Sets status to OPEN
  Sets result to NONE
  Emits MarketCreated with correct args
  Reverts on empty question
  Reverts on question > 280 chars
  Reverts if deadline < 1 hour from now
  Reverts if deadline > 365 days from now

GROUP 3 — bet (8 tests):
  Updates yesPool for YES bet
  Updates noPool for NO bet
  Records position correctly (side, amount, claimed=false)
  Emits BetPlaced with correct args
  Reverts if amount < MIN_BET
  Reverts if status != OPEN
  Reverts if past deadline (use time.increase)
  Reverts on side=0 (NONE)
  Reverts if same address bets twice on same market

GROUP 4 — closeMarket (5 tests):
  Sets CLOSED when both pools have bets
  Sets CANCELLED with "No bets placed" when empty
  Sets CANCELLED with "Only one side" when only YES bets
  Sets CANCELLED with "Only one side" when only NO bets
  Reverts if deadline not reached yet

GROUP 5 — proposeOutcome (6 tests):
  Sets status to PROPOSED
  Records proposer, proposedOutcome, proposedAt
  Records non-zero proposerSubstrateId from MockSystem
  Emits OutcomeProposed with substrate ID
  Reverts if status != CLOSED
  Reverts if msg.value != PROPOSAL_BOND
  Reverts if outcome == 0
  Reverts if proposal already exists

GROUP 6 — disputeProposal (5 tests):
  Sets disputed=true and records disputer info
  Records non-zero disputerSubstrateId from MockSystem
  Sets status to DISPUTED
  Emits OutcomeDisputed
  Reverts if challenge window passed
  Reverts if proposer tries to dispute own proposal
  Reverts if msg.value != PROPOSAL_BOND

GROUP 7 — finalizeUndisputed (4 tests):
  Resolves market with proposed outcome
  Returns PROPOSAL_BOND to proposer (verify balance change)
  Emits MarketResolved and BondReturned
  Reverts if challenge window still open
  Reverts if proposal was disputed

GROUP 8 — creatorResolveDispute (4 tests):
  Resolves correctly when proposer was right (bond returned to proposer)
  Resolves correctly when disputer was right (bond returned to disputer)
  In both cases: losing bond goes to feeRecipient
  Reverts if caller is not market creator
  Reverts if status != DISPUTED

GROUP 9 — cancelExpiredDispute (2 tests):
  Cancels and sends both bonds to fee recipient
  Reverts if creator window still open

GROUP 10 — claim (8 tests — most critical):
  Winner receives correct net payout (verify math: totalPool×share − 1%)
  Fee recipient receives exactly 1% of gross payout
  Loser reverts with "You are on the losing side"
  Cancelled market: bettor gets exact refund of bet amount
  Cancelled market: fee recipient gets nothing (no fee on refunds)
  Double claim reverts "Already claimed"
  Reverts if no position exists
  Reverts if market not resolved/cancelled
  Multiple bettors on same side receive proportional payouts (integration)

GROUP 11 — getOdds (2 tests):
  Returns 50/50 with no bets
  Returns correct split with bets (e.g., 3 YES / 1 NO = 75/25)

GROUP 12 — Full lifecycle integration tests (3 tests):
  Happy path: create → bet both sides → close → propose → finalize → claim
  Dispute path: create → bet → close → propose → dispute → creator resolves → claim
  Cancel path: single-side bets → close (auto cancel) → full refunds for all

---

## SECTION 8 — DEPLOYMENT PROCESS

Source: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/dev-environments/hardhat/

ENVIRONMENT SETUP:
  .env file with PRIVATE_KEY (never committed to git)
  Add .env to .gitignore
  Get PAS from https://faucet.polkadot.io
  Wallet needs at least 1 PAS before deploying

HARDHAT CONFIG REQUIREMENTS:
  Network name in config: polkadotTestnet
  URL: https://testnet-passet-hub-eth-rpc.polkadot.io
  chainId: 420420417
  Blockscout verification config:
    apiURL: https://blockscout-testnet.polkadot.io/api
    browserURL: https://blockscout-testnet.polkadot.io

DEPLOY SCRIPT LOGIC (in order):
  1. Get deployer signer
  2. Log deployer address and PAS balance
  3. Set SYSTEM_PRECOMPILE = 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000900
  4. Set FEE_RECIPIENT = deployer.address (or separate treasury)
  5. Deploy PolyDot(FEE_RECIPIENT, SYSTEM_PRECOMPILE)
  6. Wait for deployment confirmation
  7. Log: deployed address + explorer URL
  8. Create 3 seed markets for demo:
       "Will Polkadot Hub launch on mainnet before June 2026?" — deadline 14 days
       "Will DOT price exceed $10 before April 2026?" — deadline 7 days
       "Will this hackathon have more than 40 total submissions?" — deadline 5 days
  9. Log all created market IDs

VERIFICATION:
  Source: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/dev-environments/hardhat/#verify-a-contract
  Run hardhat verify with constructor args: FEE_RECIPIENT and SYSTEM_PRECOMPILE address
  Verified contract is important for hackathon credibility

POST-DEPLOY:
  Update CONTRACT_ADDRESS in frontend/lib/contract.ts
  Verify in Blockscout that all 3 seed markets exist

---

## SECTION 9 — FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE

Source: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/libraries/wagmi/

TECH STACK:
  Next.js 15 with App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
  wagmi v2 + viem v2 (confirmed working with Polkadot Hub)
  @tanstack/react-query v5
  Wallet: injected() connector (covers MetaMask + any EIP-1193 wallet)

WAGMI CONFIG LOGIC:
  Define custom chain via viem's defineChain:
    id: 420420417
    name: "Polkadot Hub TestNet"
    nativeCurrency: { name: "PAS", symbol: "PAS", decimals: 18 }
    rpcUrls: https://testnet-passet-hub-eth-rpc.polkadot.io
    blockExplorers: https://blockscout-testnet.polkadot.io
    testnet: true
  
  Create wagmiConfig:
    chains: [polkadotHubTestnet]
    connectors: [injected()]
    transports: http to testnet RPC

  Wrap root layout.tsx in WagmiProvider + QueryClientProvider

---

## SECTION 10 — PAGES LOGIC

### PAGE 1 — Home "/" (Market Browser)

Data: getAllMarkets() with 30s refetch interval, marketCount()

Sections:
  Hero area: project name, one-line tagline, two CTA buttons (Create Market, My Bets)
  Stats row: total markets count, "Polkadot Hub", "PVM Track 2"
  Filter tabs: All / Open / Proposed / Resolved / Cancelled
  Markets grid: 3 columns desktop, 1 mobile, each is a MarketCard
  Empty state: message + link to create

Sorting: newest first (reverse index order from getAllMarkets)

### PAGE 2 — Market Detail "/market/[id]"

This is the most important page. All interactions happen here.

Data reads (all useReadContract, refetch every 10 seconds):
  getMarket(id) → full market struct
  getProposal(id) → proposal struct
  getPosition(id, connectedAddress) → user's position
  getOdds(id) → live odds percentages

Sections rendered based on market.status:

ALWAYS VISIBLE:
  Market header: question text, status badge, market ID
  Odds bar: live YES% vs NO% with gradient
  Pool stats: YES pool, NO pool, total pool all in PAS
  Countdown timer (when OPEN and deadline in future)
  Your Position card (when user has a position)

CONDITIONAL PANELS:

status=OPEN and user has no position:
  Bet panel: amount input, YES button (green), NO button (red)
  Show potential multiplier based on current odds (informational)

status=OPEN and block.timestamp >= deadline (check client-side):
  "Close Market" button — anyone can call closeMarket()
  Explain: this transitions market to CLOSED so proposals can begin

status=CLOSED:
  Propose Outcome panel
  Explain: lock 0.05 PAS bond, 2h challenge window
  Mention: your Polkadot identity will be recorded via System Precompile
  Two buttons: Propose YES (green outline), Propose NO (red outline)

status=PROPOSED:
  Shows current proposal details: what was proposed, by whom, when
  Shows proposer's Substrate ID (formatted hex from event/storage)
  Countdown: "Challenge window closes in X"
  If user is NOT proposer: show Dispute button (orange, lock 0.05 PAS)
  If challenge window passed and not disputed: show Finalize button

status=DISPUTED:
  Shows dispute info: who disputed, their Substrate ID
  If user IS market creator: show "Resolve Dispute" with YES/NO buttons
  If user is not creator: show "Waiting for creator to resolve — Xh remaining"
  If creator window expired: show "Cancel Expired Dispute" button (anyone)

status=RESOLVED:
  Shows final result clearly (YES/NO) in large text with color
  If user bet winning side and not claimed: Claim button with estimated amount
  If user bet losing side: "Sorry, you lost" message
  If user already claimed: "Claimed ✓" badge

status=CANCELLED:
  Shows cancellation reason
  If user has unclaimed position: Claim Refund button (full refund amount)
  If no position: just shows the reason

PVM IDENTITY PANEL (show whenever proposal exists, even after resolution):
  Header: "⚡ Polkadot Identity Verification"
  Body: explains that the System Precompile (0x900) recorded these on-chain
  Shows proposerSubstrateId (formatted bytes32 hex)
  Shows disputerSubstrateId if disputed
  Label: "This identity cannot be faked — it's from Polkadot's runtime"
  This panel is important for judges to understand the PVM feature

Transaction state UX:
  All write buttons show loading spinner when tx is pending
  On success: show Blockscout link to the tx hash
  On error: show human-readable error (parse revert reason if available)
  Auto-refetch market data 5 seconds after successful tx

### PAGE 3 — Create Market "/create"

Form fields:
  Question textarea (max 280 chars, live counter below)
  Deadline selector: radio or dropdown for preset durations
    Options: 1 day / 3 days / 7 days / 14 days / 30 days
    Show the exact date/time this maps to

Info box below form:
  Three bullets explaining resolution flow
  "Cost: Free to create. Proposers stake 0.05 PAS bond."
  "Your role: You are the tiebreaker if a proposal is disputed."

Validation before tx:
  question not empty
  question length ≤ 280
  deadline valid (calculated from days selection)

On success:
  Show market ID returned from event
  Show link to the new market page
  Option to share

### PAGE 4 — Portfolio "/portfolio"

Logic:
  Get all markets from getAllMarkets()
  For each market, call getPosition(marketId, connectedAddress)
  Render only markets where user has a position OR is the creator
  Group: Active Bets / Claimable / Created Markets / History

Each row: question, market ID, your side + amount, status, WIN/LOSS/PENDING badge
Claimable rows have inline Claim button (calls claim() right from portfolio)

If wallet not connected: prompt to connect
If no positions and not a creator: show empty state with link to browse

---

## SECTION 11 — UI DESIGN SYSTEM

Judges see 37+ DeFi projects. This must look distinctive and polished.
Every design decision below is deliberate for judge impression.

COLOR TOKENS:
  background:     #09090f  (near-black, slight purple tint — feels premium)
  surface:        #111118  (card backgrounds)
  border:         #1c1c28  (subtle card borders)
  border-hover:   rgba(230,0,122,0.35) (Polkadot pink glow on hover)
  polkadot-pink:  #e6007a  (primary accent, brand color — use for CTAs)
  yes-green:      #22c55e  (green-500)
  no-red:         #ef4444  (red-500)
  text-primary:   #f1f5f9
  text-muted:     #64748b
  text-faint:     #334155

TYPOGRAPHY:
  Font: Inter or Geist (via next/font, zero external dependency)
  Numbers: use tabular-nums class so amounts don't jump when updating
  Amounts: always show 3 decimal places for PAS (e.g., "1.234 PAS")

CARD DESIGN:
  All cards: rounded-2xl, surface background, border color border
  On hover: border transitions to border-hover, scale: 1.01 (subtle lift)
  Transition: all 150ms ease-in-out
  No box-shadow — borders only

BUTTON STYLES:
  Primary (submit/bet/claim): polkadot-pink background, white text, rounded-xl, bold
  Secondary: transparent with border, text-primary, rounded-xl
  Danger (dispute): orange outline variant
  All buttons: disabled state at 50% opacity, cursor-not-allowed
  Loading state: spinner icon replaces text while tx is pending

INPUT FIELDS:
  Background: background color (#09090f)
  Border: border color (#1c1c28) normally
  Focus: border becomes polkadot-pink, no box-shadow
  Rounded-xl, consistent padding

ODDS BAR (used on every market card and detail page):
  Height: 8px
  Shape: rounded-full container
  Left fill: yes-green at yesPct% width
  Right fill: no-red at remaining width
  CSS transition: width changes animate at 500ms
  Labels above: "YES 67%" left, "NO 33%" right, bold, colored text
  Below bar: small text showing pool sizes

STATUS BADGES:
  Small pill shape: text-xs, font-bold, border, rounded-full, px-3 py-1
  OPEN:      green text, green/10 bg, green/30 border
  CLOSED:    yellow text, yellow/10 bg, yellow/30 border
  PROPOSED:  orange text, orange/10 bg, orange/30 border
  DISPUTED:  red text, red/10 bg, red/30 border
  RESOLVED:  blue text, blue/10 bg, blue/30 border
  CANCELLED: gray text, gray/10 bg, gray/30 border

COUNTDOWN TIMER:
  Shows "Xd Xh", "Xh Xm", or "Xm" depending on remaining time
  Color: text-muted normally, orange at < 1 hour, red at < 10 minutes
  Updates every 60 seconds (avoid 1-second ticks = battery drain)

LOADING STATES:
  Use skeleton cards matching the shape of MarketCard
  Pulse animation on gray placeholder shapes
  Never show blank space or raw text "Loading..."
  Show skeletons for 3 cards on initial home page load

NETWORK INDICATOR:
  Always visible in header
  Green dot + "Polkadot Hub TestNet" when on correct network
  Red warning + "Switch Network" button when on wrong network
  Switching network triggers adding Chain ID 420420417 to MetaMask

MOBILE RESPONSIVENESS:
  Home: 1 column on mobile, 2 on tablet, 3 on desktop
  Market detail: single column with panels stacking vertically
  Header: hide nav links on mobile, show hamburger or just connect button
  Test at 375px width minimum

---

## SECTION 12 — COMPONENT AND HOOK BREAKDOWN

BUILD COMPONENTS IN THIS ORDER (each depends on the previous):

LEVEL 1 — Foundation (no deps):
  OddsBar — takes yesPct: number, noPct: number, renders gradient bar
  StatusBadge — takes status: number, renders colored pill
  CountdownTimer — takes deadline: number (unix), renders live countdown
  SkeletonCard — static loading placeholder matching MarketCard shape

LEVEL 2 — Shared layout:
  Header — wallet connect/disconnect, nav links, network status indicator
  TxButton — reusable button that handles pending/success/error states uniformly
              takes onClick, disabled, loading, children props

LEVEL 3 — Market display:
  MarketCard — shows market summary, uses OddsBar + StatusBadge + CountdownTimer
               reads odds via useReadContract inside the card
               navigates to /market/[id] on click

LEVEL 4 — Market interaction:
  BetPanel — amount input + YES/NO buttons, calls useBet hook
  ProposalPanel — explain + Propose YES/NO, calls usePropose hook
  DisputePanel — shows proposal info, dispute countdown, calls useDispute hook
  CreatorResolvePanel — YES/NO resolution for market creator, calls useResolve hook
  ClaimButton — shows estimated amount, handles claim and refund, calls useClaim hook
  PVMIdentityBox — displays substrate IDs, always visible when proposal exists

LEVEL 5 — Pages:
  HomePage uses useMarkets + MarketCard grid
  MarketPage uses useMarket + usePosition + all interaction panels
  CreatePage uses writeContract directly (simple form)
  PortfolioPage uses useMarkets + getPosition for each market

HOOKS LOGIC:

useMarkets():
  Calls getAllMarkets() with refetchInterval: 30000 (30s)
  Returns markets array, isLoading, error

useMarket(id: bigint):
  Calls getMarket(id), getProposal(id), getOdds(id) in parallel
  refetchInterval: 10000 (10s)
  Returns market, proposal, odds, isLoading

usePosition(marketId: bigint, address: string | undefined):
  Calls getPosition(marketId, address) when address is defined
  Returns position, isLoading
  Automatically refetches when address changes

useBet(marketId: bigint):
  Wraps writeContractAsync for bet(marketId, side, {value})
  Returns: bet(side, amount), isPending, txHash, error
  Handles error parsing to extract revert reason

usePropose(marketId: bigint):
  Wraps writeContractAsync for proposeOutcome(marketId, outcome, {value: PROPOSAL_BOND})
  Returns: propose(outcome), isPending, txHash, error

useDispute(marketId: bigint):
  Wraps writeContractAsync for disputeProposal(marketId, {value: PROPOSAL_BOND})
  Returns: dispute(), isPending, txHash, error

useFinalize(marketId: bigint):
  Wraps writeContractAsync for finalizeUndisputed(marketId)
  Returns: finalize(), isPending, txHash, error

useResolve(marketId: bigint):
  Wraps writeContractAsync for creatorResolveDispute(marketId, outcome)
  Returns: resolve(outcome), isPending, txHash, error

useClaim(marketId: bigint):
  Wraps writeContractAsync for claim(marketId)
  Returns: claim(), isPending, txHash, error

---

## SECTION 13 — LIB FILES LOGIC

frontend/lib/wagmi.ts:
  Export: polkadotHubTestnet (custom chain definition)
  Export: wagmiConfig (createConfig result)
  Everything sourced from: https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/libraries/wagmi/

frontend/lib/contract.ts:
  Export: CONTRACT_ADDRESS (update after deploy)
  Export: POLYDOT_ABI (complete ABI array matching all functions and events)
  Export: PROPOSAL_BOND as BigInt (50000000000000000n)
  Export: MIN_BET as BigInt (1000000000000000n)
  Export: MarketStatus const object {OPEN:0, CLOSED:1, PROPOSED:2, DISPUTED:3, RESOLVED:4, CANCELLED:5}
  Export: Outcome const object {NONE:0, YES:1, NO:2}

frontend/lib/utils.ts:
  formatPAS(wei: bigint, decimals?: number) → string
    Uses viem's formatEther, then toFixed(decimals ?? 3)
    Example: 1000000000000000000n → "1.000"

  truncateAddress(address: string) → string
    Returns first 6 + "..." + last 4 chars
    Example: "0x1234...abcd"

  formatSubstrateId(id: string) → string
    bytes32 hex → first 12 + "..." + last 8 chars for display

  getStatusLabel(status: number) → string

  getStatusColor(status: number) → Tailwind class string for badge

  calculatePotentialReturn(betAmount: bigint, side: number, yesPool: bigint, noPool: bigint) → string
    Returns informational multiplier string like "~2.4x"
    If side=YES: totalIfWin = (betAmount×(yesPool+noPool+betAmount))/(yesPool+betAmount)
    Show as multiplier above 1.0
    This is an estimate — clearly labeled as such in UI

  timeUntil(timestamp: number) → string
    < 1 min: "< 1 minute"
    < 1 hour: "Xm"
    < 24 hours: "Xh Xm"
    < 7 days: "Xd Xh"
    else: "X days"

---

## SECTION 14 — ERROR HANDLING

Every user-facing error must show a human-readable message, never raw hex.

Contract reverts to handle:
  "Empty question" → "Please enter a question"
  "Question too long" → "Question must be under 280 characters"
  "Deadline too soon" → "Deadline must be at least 1 hour from now"
  "Market not open" → "This market is no longer accepting bets"
  "Deadline passed" → "Betting has closed for this market"
  "Below minimum bet" → "Minimum bet is 0.001 PAS"
  "Already bet" → "You've already placed a bet on this market"
  "Wrong bond amount" → "You must send exactly 0.05 PAS as a proposal bond"
  "Already proposed" → "An outcome has already been proposed for this market"
  "Challenge window closed" → "The challenge window has closed"
  "Proposer cannot dispute own proposal" → "You cannot dispute your own proposal"
  "Only creator" → "Only the market creator can resolve this dispute"
  "You are on the losing side" → "You bet on the wrong outcome"
  "Already claimed" → "You've already claimed your winnings"

Network errors to handle:
  User on wrong network → "Please switch to Polkadot Hub TestNet"
  RPC timeout → "Connection slow — please try again"
  User rejects tx → clear the pending state silently (don't show error)

---

## SECTION 15 — DEMO PREPARATION

SEED MARKETS CREATED IN DEPLOY SCRIPT:
  All three have deadlines far enough for judges to bet (5-14 days from deploy)
  Questions are interesting and relevant to the Polkadot ecosystem

PRE-RESOLVED DEMO MARKET:
  Create one additional market during deploy with deadline 2 hours from deploy time
  Place bets on both sides from the deployer wallet immediately after creation
  After 2 hours: closeMarket → proposeOutcome → wait 2h → finalizeUndisputed
  This gives judges a RESOLVED market to claim from on the first visit

content.content:
## Hardhat + Polkadot Hub / PVM Setup

### 1. Required NPM Packages

**For PVM (PolkaVM) development:**
```bash
npm install --save-dev @parity/hardhat-polkadot@0.2.7
npm install --save-dev @parity/resolc@1.0.0
```

**For EVM development + verification:**
```bash
npm install --save-dev @nomicfoundation/hardhat-toolbox
npm install --save-dev @nomicfoundation/hardhat-verify@^2.0.0
```

---

### 2. hardhat.config.ts — Polkadot Hub Testnet (EVM)

```ts
import type { HardhatUserConfig } from 'hardhat/config';
import '@nomicfoundation/hardhat-toolbox';
import { vars } from 'hardhat/config';

const config: HardhatUserConfig = {
  solidity: '0.8.28',
  networks: {
    polkadotTestnet: {
      url: 'https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet',
      chainId: 420420417,
      accounts: [vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY')],
    },
  },
};

export default config;
```

Set your private key via:
```bash
npx hardhat vars set PRIVATE_KEY
```

---

### 3. Contract Verification — Blockscout (no API key needed)

Add to your config:

```ts
import '@nomicfoundation/hardhat-verify';

// inside HardhatUserConfig:
etherscan: {
  apiKey: {
    polkadotTestnet: 'no-api-key-needed',
  },
  customChains: [
    {
      network: 'polkadotTestnet',
      chainId: 420420417,
      urls: {
        apiURL: 'https://blockscout-testnet.polkadot.io/api',
        browserURL: 'https://blockscout-testnet.polkadot.io/',
      },
    },
  ],
},
```

**Verify commands:**
```bash
# No constructor args
npx hardhat verify --network polkadotTestnet INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS

# With constructor args
npx hardhat verify --network polkadotTestnet INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS "arg1" "arg2"
```

---

### 4. Special Configuration Notes

**PVM project init:**
```bash
npx hardhat-polkadot init
npm install
```

**Local node requires binary setup** — download `dev-node` and `eth-rpc` binaries, then:
```bash
chmod +x /path/to/your/binary
# macOS only:
xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /path/to/your/binary
```

**Prevent local deployment hangs** — add to config:
```js
ignition: {
  requiredConfirmations: 1,
}
```

**Key compatibility caveat:** network helpers like `time.increase()` and `loadFixture` from `@nomicfoundation/hardhat-toolbox/network-helpers` are not fully compatible with Polkadot Hub's available RPCs.

input.content:
# PolyDot — Prediction Markets on Polkadot Hub

Decentralized YES/NO prediction market protocol where anyone creates questions, bets native PAS on outcomes, and resolution happens through an optimistic dispute system anchored to Polkadot's on-chain identity layer.

## Hackathon Track

**Track 2 — PVM Smart Contract**

### Why This Is Track 2

PolyDot uses two capabilities exclusive to PolkaVM and impossible on standard EVM chains:

1. **Native PAS Bets** — All bets use `msg.value` in PAS, Polkadot Hub's runtime-level native asset. No wrapped tokens, no ERC-20 intermediaries.

2. **System Precompile Identity** — The contract calls `toAccountId()` on the [System Precompile](https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/precompiles/system/) at `0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000900` to convert EVM addresses into 32-byte Polkadot Substrate AccountIds. Every proposal and dispute permanently records the participant's Polkadot identity on-chain. This is architecturally unique to PolkaVM.

## Architecture

```
contracts/
├── contracts/
│   ├── PolyDot.sol          # Main prediction market contract
│   ├── interfaces/ISystem.sol # System Precompile interface
│   └── mocks/MockSystem.sol   # Local test mock
├── test/PolyDot.test.js       # 69 tests covering all logic
├── scripts/deploy.js          # Deploy + seed 3 demo markets
└── hardhat.config.js

└── next.config.ts
```

## How It Works

1. **Create** — Anyone posts a YES/NO question with a deadline
2. **Bet** — Users bet PAS on YES or NO during the open period
3. **Close** — After deadline, anyone closes the market
4. **Propose** — Someone proposes the outcome, staking 0.05 PAS bond. Their Polkadot Substrate identity is recorded via the System Precompile
5. **Challenge** — 2-hour window for disputes (also records disputer's Substrate ID)
6. **Resolve** — If undisputed, outcome finalizes. If disputed, market creator breaks the tie
7. **Claim** — Winners claim proportional payouts (1% platform fee)

- **Integer division** — Tiny truncation remainders stay in contract (documented, expected)

# PolyDot — Prediction Markets on Polkadot Hub

Decentralized YES/NO prediction market protocol where anyone creates questions, bets native PAS on outcomes, and resolution happens through an optimistic dispute system anchored to Polkadot's on-chain identity layer.

## Hackathon Track

**Track 2 — PVM Smart Contract**

### Why This Is Track 2

PolyDot uses two capabilities exclusive to PolkaVM and impossible on standard EVM chains:

1. **Native PAS Bets** — All bets use `msg.value` in PAS, Polkadot Hub's runtime-level native asset. No wrapped tokens, no ERC-20 intermediaries.

2. **System Precompile Identity** — The contract calls `toAccountId()` on the [System Precompile](https://docs.polkadot.com/smart-contracts/precompiles/system/) at `0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000900` to convert EVM addresses into 32-byte Polkadot Substrate AccountIds. Every proposal and dispute permanently records the participant's Polkadot identity on-chain. This is architecturally unique to PolkaVM.
